critic.verdict: An exceptionally polished and comfortable Easy chart with superb rhythmic accuracy, natural foot flow, and excellent structure.

issues.problem: The JUMP LR followed immediately by a tap D at 131.013 requires a quick recovery and re-centering that might slightly challenge absolute beginners on an Easy difficulty, though it is still physically very safe.

critic.verdict: A highly creative and musically accurate chart that perfectly captures the syncopated vocal chops, held back by a single physically impossible 3-panel overlap.

issues.problem: Physically impossible step: 'tap U' at 99.871 is placed while both 'HOLD L' (99.410 - 100.102) and 'HOLD D' (99.641 - 100.102) are active, requiring three feet to execute.

critic.verdict: A solid, energetic chart with great rhythm and natural alternation, held back by minor quantization errors, an awkward vertical jump, and a locked-foot double-step.

issues.problem: While the left foot is locked holding D, the player must tap U (118.787) and then R (118.902) at 16th-note speed. This forces a fast, awkward double-step on the right foot.
A fast 16th-note transition from a DR jump into a UD (Up-Down) jump. UD jumps are vertically aligned and highly uncomfortable to execute at high speeds.
Minor quantization errors where the hold releases (at 152.490, 158.017, and 171.627) are slightly off-grid relative to the 130 BPM tempo. Snapping them to the 16th-note grid would perfect the timing.
